description: Structured specification for offline LLM-based prompt compiler. Defines operational rules for each warning type. Runtime camera meta and JSON output schema are handled separately. This version loosens trigger criteria to reduce over-conservative SAFE outputs.

FCW.name: Forward Collision Warning
FCW.description: Potential forward collision risk with a lead vehicle/obstacle or a rapid cut-in into the ego path. Prefer early warning when evidence is consistent, even if not extreme.
core_cues: Potential hazard object is in or entering the ego-path region (center corridor or projected ego lane) OR is rapidly moving into it

Closing evidence observed across frames, where closing evidence can be ANY of: noticeable size growth OR decreasing gap/relative distance impression OR forward expansion/downward shift of the object footprint
Rapid cut-in evidence: lateral intrusion toward the ego path with short time gap (may occur without clear size growth initially)
Object stays near the image centerline over time OR shows a trajectory that converges toward the ego path
temporal_behavior: Use a recent short time window (e.g., past ~2 seconds)
Trigger can be supported by as few as 2 consistent frames when the hazard is clear; otherwise prefer >=3 frames
Do not require multiple independent cues simultaneously; a single strong cue with consistency is acceptable
Suppress single-frame anomalies unless the cut-in is extreme and obvious
exclusion_rules: Ignore ego hood/dash reflections, windshield glare, and strong compression artifacts
Ignore static roadside objects clearly outside the ego path (guardrails, signs) unless they intrude into the ego path
Ignore adjacent-lane vehicles that remain stable and do not converge toward the ego path
Ignore overhead structures and far background objects with no closing evidence
tie_breaker_guidance: If cues suggest closing in the ego path and are consistent, prefer triggering FCW (tolerate some false positives)
If the object remains clearly outside the ego path and does not converge, suppress FCW
If evidence is mixed, prioritize the pattern that indicates the shortest time gap (e.g., rapid cut-in) over weaker closing cues

LDW.name: Lane Departure Warning
LDW.description: Ego vehicle drifts toward a lane boundary or crosses it while lane markings are visible and there is no clear lane-change intent. Allow pre-departure drift warnings (before crossing) when evidence persists.
core_cues: Lane markings (left/right boundary) are visible and reliable in multiple frames
Vehicle position drifts toward a lane boundary (decreasing lateral margin) with a consistent direction across frames
Lane boundary overlap/crossing is a strong trigger but not required; approaching the boundary with persistence can also trigger
Lane centerline shift relative to image center can be used as supporting evidence when boundary is visible
temporal_behavior: Prefer persistence across >=2 frames (at ~5 fps) for early drift; use >=3 frames when drift is subtle
Crossing/overlap can trigger with fewer frames if clearly observed
Do not trigger on transient jitter, single-frame lane detection noise, or brief blur/shake
exclusion_rules: Suppress when lane markings are not visible/reliable; do not infer departure purely from perspective distortion
Suppress for clear intentional lane change cues when strongly indicated (sustained steering progression + consistent lateral motion), but do not over-suppress
Suppress when road curvature explains lane center shift and the vehicle stays within boundaries
Suppress during heavy blur, strong camera shake, or severe artifacts that break lane reliability
tie_breaker_guidance: If boundary overlap/crossing is observed, prioritize LDW
If only approaching boundary is observed, require persistence and a decreasing margin trend
If intent is ambiguous but drift toward boundary is consistent, allow LDW with lower confidence rather than always SAFE

design_philosophy.FCW: Bias toward earlier warnings when consistent closing/cut-in evidence exists (lower miss tolerance).
design_philosophy.LDW: Bias toward reducing nuisance alerts, but allow pre-departure drift warnings when lane evidence is reliable.
